title: Fuzzum
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:Fuzzums-CatS.png]] |name=Fuzzum |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]{{DroidsCite|Coby and the Starhunters}}. |class=[[Bird/Legends|Avian]]{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} |subspecies= |races= |height=1.45 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]The Fuzzums stood to [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO's]] shoulder, and the droid was 1.67 meters tall according to {{DB|droid|c3po|C-3PO (See-Threepio)}}. If their longer head-feathers were to be included, they were much taller. |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=Brown |haircolor=Orange |feathers= |eyecolor=Yellow or green with brown pupils |distinctions=Flghtless; always carry spears''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' |lifespan= |origin=[[Dandelo]] |habitat= |diet= |language=[[Fuzzum language|Unidentified language]] }} {{Quote|Those Fuzzums better not mess this up!''"
"''Prince Coby! Our plan is to help our friends as well as rescue your pet!|Coby and the droids wait for the Fuzzums to create a diversion|}} The '''Fuzzums''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Bird/Legends|avians]] indigenous to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dandelo]]. Members of the species were roughly [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]], standing shorter than the average [[Human/Legends|Human]] on spindly legs. The arms and legs were hairless and brown-skinned, but the squat body was covered with orange fuzz. Fuzzums spoke their [[Fuzzum language|native language]] through a stubby, pale beak. They lived in a tribal society that relied on relatively primitive technology, characterized by simple [[Music/Legends|musical]] instruments, cliffside cave dwellings, and ubiquitous wooden [[Spear/Legends|spears]]. The Fuzzums encountered offworlders sometime before [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]]. Eventually, the avians were faced with a group known as the [[Starhunter's Intergalactic Menagerie|Starhunters]], who drove the Fuzzums from their homes in Dandelo's cliffs. The Fuzzums fled to the forests, but several of their number were captured. With the caves theirs, the Starhunters converted them into cages to hold beasts for the Starhunter's Intergalactic Menagerie. Their captive Fuzzums were forced to [[Hunting/Legends|hunt]] down new species for the Starhunters' traveling show, while those who escaped set up camps in the woods. When a new group of offworlders later arrived on Dandelo, the forest-dwelling Fuzzums assumed the worst. Nevertheless, the trio—a [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] named [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], an [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] named [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], and a [[Tammuz-an (species)/Legends|Tammuz-an]] [[Prince/Legends|prince]] named [[Coby]]—convinced the avians that they only sought to rescue a creature known as [[Ingey]] from the Starhunters. The Fuzzums and their new guests plotted and executed a [[Skirmish on Dandelo|rescue mission]] for the [[Tessellated Arboreal binjinphant|tessellated arboreal binjinphant]]. With the rescue of an old [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] named [[Greej]] and the timely arrival of [[Miner/Legends|miner]] [[Jann Tosh]], the Fuzzums and their allies freed the beasts held in the caves, drove the Starhunters from Dandelo, and reclaimed their cliff dwellings. ==Biology and appearance== [[File:FuzzumCloseup-CatS.png|thumb|left|Fuzzums had yellow eyes with brown pupils.]] The Fuzzums were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Bird/Legends|avians]] who looked like compact balls of fuzz perched upon thin, stick-like legs.''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' The arms, too, were thin and gangly, ending in three-fingered hands. While all four extremities were naked and brown-skinned, the rest of the body was covered in orange fluff. Members of the species had thick, puffed-out chests that narrowed to thin waists. Fuzzums stood about 1.45 meters tall on average. There was little distinction between where the Fuzzum torso ended and the Fuzzum head began. The avians had plastic features capable of conveying a full range of emotions. They had expressive brows over their brown-ringed eyes. These orbs were yellow with brown pupils, flanking a pale, [[Tooth/Legends|toothless]], triangular beak with a red interior and two nostrils at its apex. A shock of taller feathers adorned the top of the Fuzzum head. The overall effect made Fuzzums seem bizarre to [[Human/Legends|Humans]] and other species. ==Society and culture== {{Quote|Oh my goodness! They think we're Starhunters. They want to sell me and use Artoo for a stew pot.''"
"''What about me?''"
"''That's why they need a stew pot, I'm afraid.|First encounter between Coby, the droids, and the Fuzzums|}} [[File:Fuzzum_dwellings_on_Dandelo.png|thumb|right|Before they were driven off by the Starhunters, the Fuzzums lived in cliff dwellings on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] [[Dandelo]].]] Fuzzum society lacked the technological sophistication of much of the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The species' clothing, for example, typically consisted of little more than a cloth skirt, cords wrapped around the arms, and sandals on the feet. The characteristic design of the skirt was a white bolt of cloth with an elongated, brown, hexagonal flap in the center. They regularly wore wooden canisters attached to a baldric over one shoulder. The avians fought and [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] with simple weapons, such as [[Spear/Legends|spears]] made from wooden shafts and stone blades attached by cord—in fact, a Fuzzum and his or her spear were constant companions. Fuzzums hunted and fought in small groups. They preferred to attack with their spears, charging an enemy with the weapon held overhead and thrusting downward. They used their numbers to their advantage to overwhelm their target, and they showed no mercy to sworn enemies, such as the [[Starhunter's Intergalactic Menagerie|Starhunters]]. Captured enemies faced a dire fate: some [[Droid/Legends|droids]] could be sold, others dismantled so their parts could be put to other uses, while sentient beings could be cooked and eaten. The species was divided into tribes. Their preferred habitat was villages set into natural caves, but after the arrival of the Starhunters, the Fuzzums relocated to villages in their world's forests, where they erected lean-tos made from tree branches and strips of cloth. Fuzzums built fire pits in their villages, and they cooked stew in stew pots. Their settlements played host to festive ceremonies, during which the avians danced to the accompaniment of [[Drum/Legends|drums]], horns, and plucked string instruments. Dancing was a Fuzzum sign of joy, and boisterous laughter was a sign of amusement. Fuzzums spoke their own [[Fuzzum language|language]], which was intelligible to at least some [[Translator droid/Legends|translator droids]]. ==History== {{Quote|He says that those caves used to be their homes until the Starhunters forced them out. Now they are hunted as well. When they are caught, the Starhunters force them to help trap others.|C-3PO|}} [[File:Fuzzums_meet_the_droids.png|thumb|left|A group of Fuzzums encountered the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and the [[Tammuz-an (species)/Legends|Tammuz-an]] [[Prince/Legends|prince]] [[Coby]] in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]].]] The Fuzzums evolved on [[Dandelo]], a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of forests, savannas, and cave-pocked hills located in the [[Tammuz sector/Legends|Tammuz sector]].{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}. They achieved a basic level of technology and adopted a tribal society based around life in cliff villages. At some point between [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000]] and [[3000 BBY]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' the Fuzzums encountered strangers from another world. From this contact, the Fuzzum language was added to the repertoire of [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droids]] in the greater galaxy. Dandelo fell within the confines of [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Space]] during the [[New Sith Wars]] of [[1004 BBY|1004]] to [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], and within the confines of [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]] by [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]] and through the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Eventually, a group of offworlders came to Dandelo. The outsiders, calling themselves the Starhunters, drove the Fuzzums from their caves so as to convert the dwellings into cages to store rare beasts destined to join the traveling Starhunter's Intergalactic Menagerie. The Fuzzums escaped to Dandelo's forests, where they established temporary villages and tried to avoid Starhunter safaris on the lookout for more species to abduct. From time to time, Fuzzums were spotted and captured by the Starhunters; these captives were then forced to use their skills as trappers and hunters to find more animals for the menagerie. Over time, the Starhunters began to actively seek out Fuzzums as well as non-sentient species, and the Fuzzums grew to hate these offworlders. In [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]],{{PolyhedronCite|170|Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO}} states that [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] is set 15 years before [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of '' A New Hope'' in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], meaning that ''Star Wars: Droids'' takes place in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]]. Fuzzums from this tribe spotted a [[Starship/Legends|spacecraft]] crashing into an area of jungle. When they investigated the wreckage, they found a blue-skinned boy and two droids. Taking the trio to be Starhunters, the Fuzzums threatened to sell the golden droid, turn the shorter droid into a stew pot, and eat the boy. The golden droid knew the Fuzzum language, however, and convinced the avians that the three strangers were also enemies of the Starhunters, and only sought to rescue [[Ingey]], a captured [[Tessellated Arboreal binjinphant|tessellated arboreal binjinphant]]. The Fuzzums agreed to take the new arrivals to their former village, where the Starhunters kept their quarries. [[File:Fuzzums_attack.png|thumb|right|Upon R2-D2's signal, the Fuzzums attacked the droid guarding the animal cages, initiating the [[skirmish on Dandelo]].]] At the cliff-dwelling complex, the boy, [[Coby]], spotted his pet being thrown in a cage by a large droid. Realizing they faced some resistance, the Fuzzums and the offworlders retreated to the Fuzzums' forest village to plot their next move. The golden droid, [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], formulated a plan. A group of Fuzzums would hide near the cliffs and await a signal from the shorter droid, [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], to attack. The Fuzzums dutifully took up their position. C-3PO split off from the group and impersonated an animal known as a [[spotted jugadoo]]. The prospect of capturing the rare creature drew the Starhunters off, leaving the caves lightly guarded by [[Sentry droid/Legends|patrol droids]]. Coby and R2-D2 climbed up to the cages and freed the animals, along with an [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] [[near-Human]] man named [[Greej]]. A patrol droid found the interlopers, and a [[Skirmish on Dandelo|skirmish]] broke out in which they tussled over the droid's [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]. R2-D2 finally shot off a [[Flare/Legends|flare]], and the Fuzzums knew it was time to strike. The Fuzzums rushed in and attacked a second patrol droid with their spears. R2-D2, Coby, and Greej managed to defeat their droid and free the rest of the captives, including several Fuzzums. Reunited with their kin, the Fuzzums danced with joy. C-3PO and the Starhunters returned to the scene, chased by a jugadoo. Greej arrested the animal collectors, but the poachers managed to grab his blaster and began to make their escape. The timely arrival of the droids' master, a [[Miner/Legends|miner]] named [[Jann Tosh]] who had followed a [[Distress beacon/Legends|distress beacon]] Coby had set off before planetfall, prevented the Starhunters' escape. Thanks to the help of their offworld friends, the Fuzzums had regained their old village. They held a ceremony there to fete the offworlders who had made it possible. By [[137 ABY]], the Fuzzum [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was once again under at least the nominal control of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]] as part of their [[Hutt dependencies]]. ==Behind the scenes== The Fuzzums were created for "[[Coby and the Starhunters]]," an episode of the [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] animated television series that originally aired on [[November 2]], [[1985]]. The species has since been mentioned in various reference works, such as ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'', and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''; each of these volumes provides a nearly identical description of the species. The [[1995]] German trade paperback [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] that collects and translates [[Star Wars (1977) 4|''Star Wars'' (1977) 4]] mentions Fuzzums. In the scene where [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and his companions are in the [[Garbage Compactor 3263827|garbage compactor]] of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], the characters are trying to brace the compactor walls with poles and other objects. Solo says of the poles, "They're snapping like Fuzzum-legs." Yet, in the [[2008]] German version of ''[[Star Wars: A Long Time Ago... Volume 1: Doomworld]]'', which includes the same issue, the line is restored to, "They're snapping like twigs!" as in the original English-language version. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Fuzzums}} *{{DroidsCite|Coby and the Starhunters}} {{1st}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 4|''Star Wars'' (1977) 4]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 4}} {{Mo}} {{C|German translation only}} *''[[Star Wars Science Adventures: Journey Across Planet X]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} {{Imo}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-2|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} {{Imo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Fuzzum |nl=Fuzzum }} [[Category:Avian and airborne sentient species]] [[Category:Enslaved sentient species]] [[Category:Outer Rim Territories sentient species]]
